models.HeroContent: CMS-managed content for the landing page hero section: images, headline, subtitle, CTA buttons, and an optional notice banner.
models.Event: A public-facing trolley event with a title, date/time, description, and photo.
models.ScheduleInfo: Operating schedule, route description, and fare tiers for the Schedules & Fares page.
models.CharterInfo: Descriptive content and policies displayed on the Charters page above the request form.
models.MembershipLevel: A membership tier with name, annual cost, and list of benefits.
models.SupportOption: An additional way to support the trolley beyond membership (donate, volunteer, advertise, charter).
models.AboutContent: Organization information, recognition, officers, contact details, and hours for the About page.
models.Officer: A board member or officer with name and title.

relationships: HeroContent is a singleton managed via the CMS
Events are listed on the Events page and previewed on the landing page
MembershipLevels are displayed on the Support Us page and in the signup modal
SupportOptions are displayed below the membership section on Support Us
AboutContent is a singleton with nested officers and recognition arrays

heroContent.headline: Fort Collins Trolley
heroContent.subtitle: Ride the rails of history. Celebrating 40 years of heritage railway operation in Fort Collins.

heroImages.alt: Trolley arriving at City Park stop

noticeBanner.text: The 2026 Trolley Riding Season Starts on May 2!

events.title: Opening Day 2026
events.description: Join us for the first rides of the 2026 season! Free rides for members all day, plus live music at the depot.

events.title: Trolley History Lecture Series
events.description: Local historian Dr. Patricia Rourke presents 'Electric Dreams: How Streetcars Shaped Fort Collins' at the Mountain Avenue depot.

events.title: Kids Ride Free Day
events.description: All children 12 and under ride free with a paying adult. Special coloring books and trolley stickers for young riders.

events.title: Independence Day Celebration
events.description: Decorated trolleys run extended hours from 11am to 7pm. Enjoy patriotic decorations and festive music aboard Car 21 and Car 25.

events.title: End of Season Celebration
events.description: Last rides of the 2026 season. Member appreciation event with refreshments at the depot. Help us close out another great year on the rails.

scheduleInfo.route: City Park to Old Town (Mountain Avenue corridor, 1.5 miles)

fares.note: 6 free rides per season with membership

charterInfo.title: Charter the Trolley For an Event
charterInfo.description: A private charter can spice up a special occasion for your family, colleagues, or classroom. Charters are available outside of regular operating hours. Scheduling is limited, so please pick a preferred date and a secondary date for our crew to tentatively reserve for your event. We will confirm availability before we officially schedule and request payment to hold your date.
policies: Not available between 12–5pm on Saturdays, Sundays, or Holidays
Scheduling is subject to crew availability

membershipLevels.name: Mountain Barn Level
benefits: 10% discount on merchandise at the trolley depot

membershipLevels.name: Howes Barn Level
benefits: 10% discount on merchandise at the trolley depot

membershipLevels.name: Lifetime Membership
membershipLevels.tagline: The ultimate expression of trolley love!
benefits: 10% discount on merchandise at the trolley depot

supportOptions.title: Donate
supportOptions.description: A great way to support the Trolley is by donating. Your tax-deductible donation helps to preserve the history of the Fort Collins Municipal Railway Society.

supportOptions.title: Volunteer
supportOptions.description: We're a 100% volunteer-based organization, everything from driving and maintaining the trolleys to grant writing and website building.

supportOptions.title: Advertise Inside the Trolleys
supportOptions.description: A great way to promote your business is by advertising on a car card inside the Trolleys. The practice of advertising on streetcars started with the first horsecars in the 1890s.

supportOptions.title: Charter the Trolley For an Event
supportOptions.description: A private charter can spice up a special occasion for your family, colleagues or classroom.

aboutContent.orgDescription: The Fort Collins Municipal Railway Society (FCMRS) is a 501(c)(3) non-profit corporation working in partnership with the Fort Collins Museum to restore and operate the Fort Collins Municipal Railway, presenting a living history of electric public transportation in Fort Collins. We preserve, display, operate, and interpret streetcar service, and collect artifacts and information of an educational nature and of significance to the City's electric railway.
missionPoints: Promote excellence in operating safely and in strict accordance with our Operating Agreement with the City.
Adhere to all applicable federal, state and local laws.
Maintain the equipment, power supply and track in safe, attractive and functioning condition and as close to the historically accurate, original condition as practical.
volunteerRoles: Elected officers and board of directors
recognition: Department of Transportation Award for Outstanding Public Service to Transportation and Historic Preservation
Award of Merit from the American Association of State and Local History
Preservation Honor Award from Colorado Preservation, Inc.
Stephen H. Hart Award from the Colorado Historical Society
Designated as a Fort Collins Local Landmark and entered in the National Register of Historic Places
aboutContent.hours: Saturdays, Sundays, & Holidays | Noon - 5pm | May - Sept.

paymentOptions.checkMailTo: 330 N. Howes Street, Fort Collins, CO 80521
